Title: Site-specific phosphorylation of TRANSPARENT TESTA GLABRA1 mediates carbon partitioning in Arabidopsis seeds

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native TTG1 promoter in genomic rescue constructs (g4HA-TTG1, g4HA-TTG1S215E, g4HA-TTG1S215A; constitutive in native seed expression context)
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0 near-isogenic background; ttg1-13 introgressed into Col-0
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: TTG1 is a promising engineering node for tuning seed carbon partitioning between embryo oil and maternal seed coat products. Editing or protein engineering at Ser215, or stacking with upstream SK11/SK12 modulation, could increase seed fatty acids while reducing seed coat mucilage and flavonoid pigments; conversely blocking phosphorylation could favor seed coat protective traits. Deployment should consider maternal control of the trait and pleiotropic effects on testa color/mucilage.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TTG1 acts as a phosphorylation-sensitive switch in seeds: unphosphorylated TTG1 promotes mucilage production, columella formation, proanthocyanidin/flavonoid pigmentation, and GL2 expression, while repressing seed fatty acid accumulation; phosphorylation-mimic TTG1S215E shows the opposite trend.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant analysis (ttg1-13); genomic complementation; phosphomimic/dephosphomimic transgenes (S215E/S215A); in vitro kinase assay; Phos-tag immunoblot for in vivo phosphorylation; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; pull-down; co-immunoprecipitation; ChIP; EMSA-supported recruitment via TT2; qRT-PCR; seed metabolite/phenotype analyses
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master switch for seed carbon partitioning between embryo fatty acid biosynthesis and maternal seed coat mucilage/flavonoid production, with activity determined by Ser215 phosphorylation status.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing siliques/seeds, especially 0–4 DAP and analyses at 4 DAP; bikinin treatment of siliques inhibits upstream GSK3-like kinases and alters TTG1 phosphorylation/signaling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: TTG1 expression peaks in developing seeds around 4 DAP; strongly expressed in maternal seed tissues including chalaza and integument
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Peaks in developing seeds around 4 DAP; strong in maternal seed tissues including chalaza and integument
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Carbon partitioning among fatty acid biosynthesis in the embryo and flavonoid pigment/proanthocyanidin plus mucilage production in the seed coat
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS interactome/proteomics for TTG1 pull-down; phosphosite LC-MS/MS; ProteomeXchange/PRIDE: PXD008609
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TTG1 is a WD40 regulator rather than a DNA-binding TF; its recruitment to GL2 is partially dependent on TT2. Ser215 phosphorylation specifically affects seed traits but not post-embryonic epidermal traits such as trichome and root hair patterning. Seed coat defects of TTG1S215E were only partially rescued, suggesting additional phosphorylation-independent functions.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increasing oil through TTG1 phosphorylation mimic or reduced TTG1 activity is accompanied by paler seeds, reduced proanthocyanidin/flavonoid deposition, reduced mucilage extrusion, and defective columellae; blocking phosphorylation decreases oil.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in MYB-bHLH-WD40 regulatory complexes; experimentally supported interaction with TT2 in seeds; TTG1-TT2 complex associates with GL2 locus
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WD40 repeat regulatory protein; WD40 beta-propeller
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein-protein interaction-dependent transcriptional co-regulation; recruitment to GL2 chromatin; activity modulated by phosphorylation at Ser215
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes GL2 expression when unphosphorylated and able to interact with TT2; phosphorylation at Ser215 weakens GL2 association and reduces GL2 expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TTG1 is phosphorylated in vitro and in vivo by SK11/SK12, mainly at Ser215. 2. Unphosphorylated TTG1 interacts with TT2, is recruited to the GL2 locus, and promotes GL2 expression, leading to more seed coat mucilage and flavonoid pigments/proanthocyanidins but less seed fatty acid accumulation. 3. Phosphorylation-mimicking TTG1S215E abolishes TT2 interaction, weakens GL2 binding, downregulates GL2, and shifts carbon flow toward seed fatty acid synthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Seed fatty acids (with reciprocal effects on seed coat proanthocyanidin/flavonoid pigments and mucilage)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Embryo for fatty acids; seed coat for mucilage and proanthocyanidins/flavonoid pigments
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed storage reserves for germination and seedling growth; flavonoid pigments and mucilage contribute to seed coat protection, hydration, dormancy/germination balance, and stress buffering
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: SK11/SK12 kinase activity; bikinin-sensitive GSK3-like kinase signaling; seed developmental stage

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: SK11 native promoter (2.6-kb upstream) for SK11-GFP and gain-of-function SK11E292K-GFP constructs; SK11:GUS transcriptional fusion (native/constitutive developmental promoter context)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Increasing SK11 activity can raise seed fatty acid content, whereas reducing SK11 function can enhance seed coat mucilage and flavonoid pigmentation. SK11 is suitable for engineering carbon partitioning upstream of TTG1, especially together with TTG1 phosphosite manipulation, but maternal effects and testa-quality penalties must be considered.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SK11 promotes seed fatty acid accumulation but suppresses seed coat mucilage, columella formation, and proanthocyanidin/flavonoid pigment deposition by phosphorylating TTG1.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TTG1 pull-down followed by LC-MS/MS; GST pull-down; BiFC; yeast two-hybrid; in vivo co-immunoprecipitation; null mutant/double mutant and gain-of-function SK11E292K transgenic analyses; qRT-PCR; Phos-tag kinase assay; reciprocal crosses; bikinin pharmacology
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major upstream kinase that shifts seed carbon flow toward embryo oil accumulation and away from maternal seed coat products through TTG1 phosphorylation.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Highly relevant in young siliques/developing seeds, especially 0–4 DAP and 4 DAP; bikinin treatment inhibits SK11-related GSK3 kinase activity
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in many aerial tissues, highest in young siliques; high during 0–4 DAP in developing seeds; strong expression in chalaza and integument of 4-DAP seeds and in whole seeds at 2 DAP
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in many aerial tissues, highest in young siliques; high during 0–4 DAP in developing seeds; strong in chalaza and integument
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Carbon partitioning controlling embryo fatty acid biosynthesis versus seed coat mucilage and flavonoid/proanthocyanidin production
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS interactome/proteomics identified SK11 as TTG1 interactor; phosphosite LC-MS/MS on phosphorylated TTG1; ProteomeXchange/PRIDE: PXD008609
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SK11 is also known as ASKα. Gain-of-function E292K in the conserved TREE motif increased protein stability. Reciprocal crosses showed maternal control over seed fatty acid phenotype. Discussion cites prior reports that SK11 can phosphorylate G6PD in other contexts, but this was not the core mechanism demonstrated here.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced SK11 activity raises seed fatty acids but causes paler seeds, lower PA/flavonoid deposition, reduced mucilage extrusion, and smaller/collapsed columellae; reduced SK11 activity lowers oil.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TTG1 (phosphorylation target); indirectly affects GL2 via TTG1-TT2 module
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Direct kinase-substrate interaction with TTG1; no stable larger complex conclusively defined beyond TTG1-associated regulatory module
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Phosphorylation activation of downstream signaling output; site-specific phosphorylation of TTG1 at Ser215
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly downregulates GL2 by phosphorylating TTG1 and disrupting TTG1-TT2-dependent recruitment to GL2
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SK11 physically interacts with TTG1 and is highly expressed in young seeds, particularly maternal seed tissues. 2. SK11 phosphorylates TTG1 mainly at Ser215; gain-of-function SK11E292K elevates seed fatty acids and reduces mucilage/pigmentation, whereas loss of SK11 together with SK12 has opposite effects. 3. SK11 acts genetically upstream of TTG1/TT2 and indirectly suppresses GL2 expression to redirect carbon toward fatty acid synthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Seed fatty acids (with reciprocal effects on seed coat proanthocyanidins/flavonoid pigments and mucilage)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Embryo for fatty acids; seed coat for mucilage and proanthocyanidins/flavonoid pigments
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Storage oil for germination and nutrition; seed coat mucilage/pigments for protection and germination-related functions
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Seed developmental program; bikinin-sensitive GSK3 kinase network; specific upstream signal not established in this paper

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: SK12 native promoter (2.3-kb upstream) for SK12:GUS transcriptional fusion; endogenous locus for mutant analysis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: SK12 can be targeted together with SK11 to modulate maternal control of carbon allocation in seeds. Reducing SK12 activity together with SK11 may enhance mucilage and flavonoid pigments but lower oil; enhancing activity is expected to favor oil accumulation via TTG1 phosphorylation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SK12 functions redundantly with SK11 to promote seed fatty acid accumulation and suppress seed coat mucilage, columella formation, and flavonoid/proanthocyanidin deposition through TTG1 phosphorylation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GST pull-down; BiFC; yeast two-hybrid; null mutant/double mutant analysis; qRT-PCR; Phos-tag in vitro kinase assay; pharmacological support with bikinin via subgroup I/II GSK inhibition
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Redundant upstream kinase partner of SK11 required for the full regulatory switch that directs carbon toward embryo oil instead of seed coat products.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High relevance in young siliques/developing seeds, especially 0–4 DAP; bikinin-sensitive kinase pathway
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Much higher in rosette leaves and young siliques than in other aerial tissues; high from 0–4 DAP in developing seeds; strong in whole 2-DAP seeds and in chalaza/integument at 4 DAP
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in rosette leaves and young siliques; high during early seed development with strong chalaza and integument expression
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Carbon partitioning controlling embryo fatty acid biosynthesis versus seed coat mucilage and flavonoid/proanthocyanidin production
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Phosphosite LC-MS/MS on phosphorylated TTG1 supported kinase-site assignment in combination with kinase assays; no separate accession beyond PRIDE study dataset PXD008609
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single sk12 mutant was similar to wild type, but sk11 sk12 double mutant showed strong phenotypes, indicating redundancy. The study centers on SK11/SK12 together rather than SK12 alone.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: As part of the SK11/SK12 module, higher activity is associated with increased oil but reduced seed coat protective traits; loss contributes to reduced oil.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TTG1 (phosphorylation target, mainly Ser215); indirectly affects GL2 via TTG1-TT2 module
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Direct kinase-substrate interaction with TTG1 in the regulatory module
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly represses GL2 output through phosphorylation-dependent disruption of TTG1 function
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SK12 physically interacts with TTG1 and is highly expressed during early seed development in maternal tissues. 2. SK12 phosphorylates TTG1 mainly at Ser215 in vitro and functions redundantly with SK11 in vivo. 3. Together with SK11, SK12 promotes seed fatty acid accumulation while suppressing seed coat mucilage and flavonoid/proanthocyanidin production.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Seed fatty acids (with reciprocal effects on seed coat proanthocyanidins/flavonoid pigments and mucilage)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Embryo for fatty acids; seed coat for mucilage and proanthocyanidins/flavonoid pigments
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Storage reserve and seed coat protective functions
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Seed developmental program; bikinin-sensitive GSK3 kinase network; specific upstream signal not established in this paper

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for TT2-GFP overexpression in interaction/ChIP assays; endogenous TT2 locus in mutant tt2-5
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: TT2 is a useful regulator for manipulating seed coat flavonoid pigmentation and mucilage, but it inversely affects seed fatty acid accumulation through the TTG1-GL2 module. It could be stacked with TTG1 phosphosite edits for finer control of seed carbon allocation, with attention to maternal seed coat effects.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TT2 promotes seed coat mucilage, columella formation, proanthocyanidin/flavonoid pigment deposition, and GL2 expression, while reducing seed fatty acid accumulation; tt2 suppresses sk11 sk12 seed phenotypes, placing TT2 downstream of SK11/SK12 together with TTG1.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (tt2-5 and tt2-5 sk11 sk12); expression profiling; yeast two-hybrid; pull-down; BiFC; in vivo co-immunoprecipitation with TTG1 variants; ChIP-qPCR at GL2; EMSA on GL2 AC-rich motif; qRT-PCR of GL2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential MYB partner that recruits TTG1 function toward GL2 activation, thereby favoring maternal seed coat product formation over embryo oil accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seeds/siliques, especially around 4 DAP; seed developmental stage dependency
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Temporal expression in developing seeds resembles TTG1, peaking at the embryo morphogenesis phase around 4 DAP; predominantly associated with seed coat according to prior work cited in discussion
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Peaks in developing seeds around 4 DAP; mainly seed coat-associated
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TTG1; likely TT8 in canonical MBW complexes from prior literature, but this paper directly validates TTG1 interaction
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional control of carbon partitioning between seed coat mucilage/flavonoid-proanthocyanidin production and embryo fatty acid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TT2 binds an AC-rich motif near the GL2 transcription start site; TTG1 itself does not bind DNA in EMSA, but is recruited via interaction with TT2. TTG1 Ser215 phosphorylation abolishes TT2 interaction, making TT2 central to phosphoregulation of the module.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhancing TT2-associated activity is expected to increase seed coat pigmentation and mucilage but reduce oil accumulation; tt2 causes transparent testa, reduced PA deposition, and lower mucilage-related traits.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a TT2-TTG1 regulatory complex at the GL2 locus; broader MBW complex context is discussed but not the central focus here
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via direct DNA binding to GL2 promoter region and recruitment of TTG1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GL2; recruits/stabilizes TTG1 association with GL2 locus
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TT2 acts genetically downstream of SK11/SK12 and is required for the sk11 sk12 effects on seed coat color and fatty acid levels. 2. TT2 directly binds the GL2 locus and promotes TTG1 recruitment there. 3. Phosphorylation of TTG1 at Ser215 abolishes TTG1-TT2 interaction, thereby reducing GL2 expression and shifting carbon to seed fatty acids.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Seed coat proanthocyanidins/flavonoid pigments and mucilage, inversely associated with seed fatty acids
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed coat for proanthocyanidins/flavonoid pigments and mucilage; embryo for reciprocal fatty acid effect
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed coat protection, pigmentation, hydration/dormancy balance, and reserve partitioning
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: TTG1 interaction state; SK11/SK12-mediated TTG1 phosphorylation indirectly modulates TT2 complex formation; seed developmental stage

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous GL2 locus examined; no transgenic promoter manipulation for GL2 reported as a core experiment in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: GL2 is a downstream transcriptional lever for modifying seed carbon allocation. Increasing GL2 expression is expected to favor seed coat mucilage/flavonoid traits and reduce oil, whereas decreasing GL2 may raise oil but compromise seed coat properties. It could be targeted downstream of the SK11/SK12-TTG1-TT2 module.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GL2 expression positively correlates with seed coat mucilage/columella and flavonoid/proanthocyanidin traits, and negatively with seed fatty acid accumulation in the regulatory framework described.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-qPCR showing TTG1 and TT2 association with GL2 locus; EMSA showing TT2 binding to GL2 AC-rich motif and TTG1 supershift through TT2; qRT-PCR of GL2 in multiple genetic backgrounds and after bikinin treatment
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Immediate downstream transcriptional output of the phospho-TTG1/TT2 module that determines whether carbon is directed to seed coat products or embryo fatty acids.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing siliques at 4 DAP; bikinin treatment upregulates GL2 in wild type by inhibiting GSK3-like kinases
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In developing seeds, GL2 expression gradually rises during embryo morphogenesis and peaks around 6 DAP before maturation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Gradually increases in developing seeds and peaks around 6 DAP
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TT2; TTG1 (chromatin-associated regulatory recruitment rather than direct DNA binding by TTG1)
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Downstream transcriptional control of seed coat mucilage/flavonoid formation versus embryo fatty acid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper primarily analyzes GL2 as a downstream target rather than the original discovery gene. The gl2 mutant phenotype is referenced and shown in supplementary data to support the model, but the main mechanistic evidence here is regulatory binding and expression change.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher GL2 activity is associated with stronger seed coat traits but lower oil; lower GL2 favors oil accumulation but compromises mucilage, columella integrity, and pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Target locus for TT2-TTG1 regulatory complex recruitment
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional output gene activated by TT2 and TTG1-associated complex; expression suppressed when TTG1 is phosphorylated by SK11/SK12
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GL2 is directly associated with TT2 and TTG1 in developing siliques, with strongest binding near the transcription start site. 2. TTG1 phosphorylation at Ser215 weakens TTG1 recruitment to GL2 by disrupting TTG1-TT2 interaction, while TT2 binding remains TTG1-independent. 3. GL2 expression is downregulated in ttg1, tt2, and SK11 gain-of-function conditions, but upregulated in sk11 sk12 and after bikinin treatment, linking GL2 to seed carbon partitioning.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Seed coat mucilage and flavonoid/proanthocyanidin pigments, inversely associated with seed fatty acids
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed coat for mucilage and proanthocyanidins/flavonoid pigments; reciprocal oil effect in embryo
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed coat protection, hydration, pigmentation, dormancy/germination-related functions, and carbon allocation control
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: TT2; TTG1; SK11/SK12-dependent TTG1 phosphorylation state; bikinin-sensitive GSK3 kinase signaling
